mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or flat at a restrictive level, equity discount rates ease relative to prior months and S&P 500 prices re-rate higher over the next month. The signal is confirmed if lower policy rates align with positive 20-day returns and rejected if rate cuts occur without a positive equity response.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines or stays unchanged for at least one month but the S&P 500 has negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of such observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL acceleration above recent trend raises expected policy pressure and real discount rates, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is still above the prior 6-month average.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation accelerations above the threshold are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the return response is not negative within any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply from its recent level, labor-market deterioration signals weaker household income and demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is a recession-warning effect rather than a growth effect.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases above the trigger and the S&P 500 still posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS after a weak period signals improving nominal consumer spending and corporate revenue prospects, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails when sales growth is driven only by price inflation rather than real demand.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth turns positive after at least 2 months of nonpositive growth
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth episodes do not produce above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal disappears once CPIAUCSL acceleration is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and capacity utilization, which usually accompanies improving profit expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is stronger when output growth is broad rather than one-off.
failure_condition: Reject if months with positive INDPRO momentum are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained downturn in HOUST signals weakening residential investment and softer cyclical demand, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect should appear only after a clear break below recent housing activity.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% versus its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing declines beyond the threshold are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, payroll contraction and higher unemployment jointly identify a broad labor-market deterioration that should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The joint condition matters because payroll losses without unemployment increases are weaker recession evidence.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls over 2 months while UNRATE rises over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-loss and unemployment-rise condition does not produce negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates real activity and nominal spending are both strengthening, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if GDP growth is positive but retail sales contract, because that implies weak consumer transmission to earnings.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise versus their prior readings
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP and retail-sales expansion is not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the effect disappears when one of the two series is removed.
